description: Scriveno constraint system: work types, command availability, exports, and dependencies. Every command checks this file at runtime.

_notes.sacred_keys: Sacred subcommands live at commands/scr/sacred/<name>.md and run as /scr:sacred:<name>. Their CONSTRAINTS keys use the sacred:<name> form so /scr:help can render the runnable slash-command path directly. The sacred-numbering-format entry is a separate flat command (commands/scr/sacred-numbering-format.md) that surfaces the active tradition's numbering format. It used to be named sacred-verse-numbering, which collided with sacred:verse-numbering at install time -- both flattened to scr-sacred-verse-numbering. Renamed in v1.6.x; the installer now refuses to install when two sources share a flat skill name.

structure.label: Structure
structure.description: Use /scr:outline as the hub for outline shape, then use specialist commands for adding, inserting, removing, splitting, merging, and reordering units.

art.label: Art
art.description: Use /scr:art-direction as the visual hub, then branch to cover, scene, character, map, storyboard, panel, and spread prompts.

session.label: Session
session.description: Use /scr:save as the session checkpoint hub, with history, versions, compare, pause, resume, progress, and reports as nearby state tools.

sacred.label: Sacred
sacred.description: Use /scr:sacred:source-tracking as the citation and tradition hub, then branch to concordance, cross-reference, genealogy, chronology, annotation, verse numbering, numbering format, and doctrinal checks.

submission.label: Submission
submission.description: Use /scr:publish as the destination wizard, then branch to synopsis, query letter, book proposal, discussion questions, prepublish review, and submission packaging.

publishing.label: Publishing
publishing.description: Use /scr:publish for sequencing, /scr:export for one-off output, and the build commands for final platform packages.

world.label: World
world.description: Use /scr:build-world as the world hub, then branch to characters, peoples, places, geography, relationships, cast lists, and research.

collaboration.label: Collaboration
collaboration.description: Use /scr:track as the collaboration hub for alternate versions, proposals, comparisons, merges, and save history.

surface.label: Command Surface
surface.description: Use /scr:surface to inspect, shrink, preview, or restore the visible installed command profile without deleting project data.

scripture_biblical.label: Scripture (Biblical)
scripture_biblical._note: Varies by scope -- full Bible vs single book. No meaningful total range.

scripture_quranic.label: Scripture (Quranic)
scripture_quranic._note: Varies by scope -- full Quran vs single surah. No meaningful total range.

scripture_torah.label: Scripture (Torah)
scripture_torah._note: Varies by scope -- full Torah vs single parashah.

scripture_vedic.label: Scripture (Vedic)
scripture_vedic._note: Varies by scope -- single Veda vs collection.

scripture_buddhist.label: Scripture (Buddhist)
scripture_buddhist._note: Varies by scope -- single sutta vs full nikaya.

discuss.description: Shape the next unit before planning

plan.description: Research and plan the next unit

editor-review.description: Manual review of drafted unit and editor-writer collaboration workflow

next.description: Auto-detect and run the next workflow step

first-run.description: Guided first-run path through proof, demo, runtime checks, and starter choices

do.description: Natural language router for free-text commands

help.description: Show available commands grouped by stage

progress.description: Show current state and next step

import.description: Import existing manuscript into Scriveno structure

map-manuscript.description: Analyze existing manuscript structure, voice, characters, and themes

new-people.description: Build a people (race, faction, culture, nation) as a collective entity, the tier above individual characters

new-place.description: Create a confirmed place profile in PLACES.md through guided questions

place-touch.description: Update a place's evolving geography, appearances, research status, or continuity rules

geography-map.description: Render or regenerate the derived geography map from PLACES.md and the world surface

climax.description: Generate and pressure-test the story's climax where the central conflict, the crisis, character arcs, and planted payoffs converge

subject-touch.description: Update evolving subject, concept, procedure, doctrine, object, image, or reader-state movement

beta-reader.description: Simulate a beta reader's experience of the manuscript using a fresh-context reader persona (same model, not a second or external AI).

prepublish-review.description: Run a final editorial go/no-go review before export or publishing

publish.description: Publishing wizard or preset-driven pipeline (wraps export commands)

export.description: Compile and export manuscript to publication-ready formats (markdown, docx, pdf, epub, fountain, fdx, latex, packages)

autopilot-publish.description: Run full publishing pipeline unattended with quality gate

build-ebook.description: Build EPUB with platform-aware accessibility guardrails (Pandoc + EAA compliance)

build-print.description: Build print-ready PDF with platform trim sizes and page-count guardrails (Pandoc + Typst), and academic publisher class wrappers for IEEE, ACM, Springer LNCS, Elsevier, and APA7 (Pandoc + LaTeX).

build-smashwords.description: Build Smashwords/D2D-compliant DOCX via Pandoc --reference-doc (no tabs, first-line indent via style, auto-TOC)

build-poetry-submission.description: Build poetry submission manuscript DOCX (one poem per page, 12pt Times/Garamond, title page, TOC)

translate.description: Translate manuscript per-unit using fresh-context-per-unit pattern with glossary and translation memory

translation-glossary.description: Create and manage bilingual term glossary for consistent translation

translation-memory.description: Build and manage translation memory from completed translations

cultural-adaptation.description: Flag idioms, humor, customs, and cultural references that need localization

back-translate.description: Translate translation back to source language for side-by-side verification

multi-publish.description: Export translated editions in all target formats with localized front/back matter

autopilot-translate.description: Run multi-language translation pipeline unattended with glossary, translation, and publishing

manager.description: Interactive command center for managing multiple writing projects

health.description: Diagnose and repair common project state issues

surface.description: Inspect or change the installed Scriveno command profile

proof-unit.description: Run one manuscript unit through a proof path from voice profile to export readiness

scan.description: Detect drift between recorded state (STATE.md, OUTLINE.md, config.json) and what is on disk

sync.description: Synchronize installed Scriveno runtime commands, skills, and agents with the current source

cleanup.description: Strip template scaffold markers from draft files

validate.description: Scan manuscript for unresolved scaffold markers before export

fast.description: Make a quick inline edit without full planning overhead

research.description: Add sourced advisory research notes for any topic without changing project canon

add-note.description: Add a quick note or reminder to the project notes file

plant-seed.description: Plant a creative seed -- an idea, image, or fragment for future use

troubleshoot.description: Diagnose why something isn't working and suggest fixes

thread.description: Start or continue a focused conversation thread on a specific topic

track.description: Manage revision tracks -- create, switch, compare, merge, and propose changes

build-world.description: System boundaries, environments, and operating context

plot-graph.description: Procedure flow, escalation path, and task sequence

beta-reader.description: Operator or reader trial for task completion

character-ref.description: Visual figure reference for sacred art

tradition.description: The specific sacred tradition profile slug for the work

verse_numbering_system.description: Verse/passage numbering convention for the tradition

calendar_system.description: Calendar system used for dates and chronology

canonical_alignment.description: Canonical translation to align vocabulary and phrasing with

annotation_traditions.description: Annotation traditions to apply or reference

preserve_source_terms.description: Terms that should never be translated, kept in source language with transliteration

transliteration_style.description: Style of transliteration for non-Latin script terms

surface_applicability._description: Decision tree for the variable .manuscript context surfaces (BRIEF, CHARACTERS, RELATIONSHIPS, WORLD, PLOT-GRAPH, THEMES, PEOPLES). Canonical names map to adapted filenames via file_adaptations. Universal files (WORK, OUTLINE, RECORD, STATE, PROGRESS, STYLE-GUIDE, WRITING-RULES) are always created and are not governed here. /scr:new-work creates every surface marked required or optional and skips not_applicable; /scr:help surfaces which apply. by_group is the default per work-type group; work_type_overrides is a partial patch over the group default for a specific type (a surface named in an override moves to that tier and is removed from the others).

architectural_profiles._description: Phase 29 v1.7: valid values + work-type defaults for the project-spec tradition and platform keys. The runtime validator cross-checks these values against the templates/sacred/ and templates/platforms/ directory listings -- the intersection is the accepted set, so new contributor-dropped manifests are picked up at load time without editing this file.

_render_variants._note: How templates/WORK.md {{PROFILE_BLOCK}} is rendered per work-type group by /scr:new-work. Wiring of new-work substitution lands in a later phase.
_render_variants.script: Not applicable for this work type.
_render_variants.academic: Not applicable for this work type.
_render_variants.technical: Not applicable for this work type.
_render_variants.interactive: Not applicable for this work type.
_render_variants.speech_song: Not applicable for this work type.

traditions._note: This array seeds the initial set. The authoritative runtime value list is the intersection of _seeded with directory names under templates/sacred/ that contain a manifest.yaml. A contributor dropping templates/sacred/<new-tradition>/manifest.yaml extends the accepted set without editing this file.

platforms._note: This array seeds the initial set. The authoritative runtime value list is the intersection of _seeded with directory names under templates/platforms/ that contain a manifest.yaml. A contributor dropping templates/platforms/<new-platform>/manifest.yaml extends the accepted set without editing this file.

defaults_by_work_type._description: Per-work-type defaults applied by the runtime when the project spec omits the key. The value MUST be one of the seeded traditions/platforms above. An unlisted work type MUST NOT receive a default (runtime returns null).

messages.command_hidden: The `{command}` command is designed for {available_groups}. Your {work_type} uses `{alternative}` instead.
messages.prerequisite_missing: You need to run `{prerequisite}` before `{command}`. Want me to run it now?
messages.prerequisite_file_missing: The `{command}` command needs `{file}` which doesn't exist yet. Run `{generator_command}` first.
messages.command_adapted: Note: in your {work_type} project, `{original}` is called `{adapted}`. Both names work.
